narrative: As an Asian male applicant with a solid undergraduate GPA of 3.50—above the typical 3.21 average—your law school application stood out in some academic areas, but the admissions model ultimately predicted a 48% chance of failing the bar exam on your first try, tipping the decision toward rejection to minimize risks for the program. This prediction draws from patterns in data on thousands of past students, focusing on factors like early grades, later performance, and test scores that best forecast bar success. The top influences pushing the model toward this "fail" prediction were your Year 1 school decile ranking of 1st—well below the average 6th decile—which signaled a weaker start compared to peers who typically thrive on the bar. Close behind was your Year 3 decile of 2nd, again lower than the average 6th, suggesting inconsistent upward momentum that the model sees as a red flag for the intense bar prep ahead. While your upper-middle family income and full-time study plans align with the majority of students, they didn't outweigh these early grade concerns. Interestingly, your stronger undergrad GPA pulled in the opposite direction, nudging the prediction toward bar passage and showing your potential outside of law-specific coursework. Compared to typical applicants who pass the bar, your LSAT score of 31 falls short of the 37 average, adding subtle pressure, though it wasn't the dominant factor here. These early decile rankings mattered most because they capture foundational performance that strongly correlates with bar outcomes in the data. Your situation reflects many high-achieving Asian students who excel in undergrad but face hurdles in law school grading curves. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT to boost it toward 35+, strengthening your Year 1 and 3 deciles through targeted prep courses, or applying to programs with flexible admissions that value your GPA edge. Gathering letters highlighting your resilience could also help reframe your profile for holistic reviews. With focused improvements, your profile has real upside for future applications.
